2603.13691.title: QuarkMedBench: A Real-World Scenario Driven Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models
2603.13691.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) excel on standardized medical exams, high scores often fail to translate to high-quality responses for real-world medical queries. Current evaluations rely heavily on multiple-choice questions, failing to capture the unstructured, ambiguous, and long-tail complexities inherent in genuine user inquiries. To bridge this gap, we introduce QuarkMedBench, an ecologically valid benchmark tailored for real-world medical LLM assessment. We compiled a massive dataset spanning Clinical Care, Wellness Health, and Professional Inquiry, comprising 20,821 single-turn queries and 3,853 multi-turn sessions. To objectively evaluate open-ended answers, we propose an automated scoring framework that integrates multi-model consensus with evidence-based retrieval to dynamically generate 220,617 fine-grained scoring rubrics (~9.8 per query). During evaluation, hierarchical weighting and safety constraints structurally quantify medical accuracy, key-point coverage, and risk interception, effectively mitigating the high costs and subjectivity of human grading. Experimental results demonstrate that the generated rubrics achieve a 91.8% concordance rate with clinical expert blind audits, establishing highly dependable medical reliability. Crucially, baseline evaluations on this benchmark reveal significant performance disparities among state-of-the-art models when navigating real-world clinical nuances, highlighting the limitations of conventional exam-based metrics. Ultimately, QuarkMedBench establishes a rigorous, reproducible yardstick for measuring LLM performance on complex health issues, while its framework inherently supports dynamic knowledge updates to prevent benchmark obsolescence.
2603.13691.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在標準化醫學考試中表現優異，但高分往往無法轉化為對現實醫學問題的高質量回應。當前的評估過於依賴選擇題，未能捕捉到真正用戶查詢中固有的非結構化、模糊和長尾複雜性。為了彌補這一差距，我們推出了QuarkMedBench，一個為現實醫學LLM評估量身定制的生態有效基準。我們編輯了一個龐大的數據集，涵蓋臨床護理、健康福祉和專業查詢，包括20,821個單回合查詢和3,853個多回合會話。為了客觀評估開放式答案，我們提出了一個自動評分框架，將多模型共識與基於證據的檢索相結合，動態生成220,617個細緻的評分標準（每個查詢約9.8個）。在評估過程中，層級加權和安全約束結構性地量化醫學準確性、關鍵點覆蓋和風險攔截，有效減輕了人為評分的高成本和主觀性。實驗結果表明，生成的評分標準與臨床專家盲審的符合率達到91.8%，建立了高度可靠的醫學可靠性。至關重要的是，對這一基準的基線評估顯示，當面對現實臨床細微差別時，最先進模型之間存在顯著的性能差異，突顯了傳統考試基準的局限性。最終，QuarkMedBench建立了一個嚴謹、可重複的標準，用於衡量LLM在複雜健康問題上的表現，同時其框架本質上支持動態知識更新，以防止基準過時。
2603.13691.author: Yao Wu et.al.
2603.13691.authors: Yao Wu, Kangping Yin, Liang Dong, Zhenxin Ma, Shuting Xu, Xuehai Wang, Yuxuan Jiang, Tingting Yu, Yunqing Hong, Jiayi Liu, Rianzhe Huang, Shuxin Zhao, Haiping Hu, Wen Shang, Jian Xu, Guanjun Jiang
